author.name: Justin Chen and Allen Fan
author.tagline: Computational Neuroscience · Biomedical Engineering · Machine Learning

hero.title: EEG-Guided Neural Scaffold Design
hero.subtitle: Using Functional Connectivity Analysis to Design Patient-Specific Post-Stroke Brain Repair Architectures
hero.hook: The first computational pipeline that fuses EEG, DTI, and MRI to generate 3D-printable neural scaffolds with anatomically curved microchannels — sized by how functionally critical each connection is.
hero.metadata: CSEF / ISEF 2027 · Biomedical Engineering · Justin Chen and Allen Fan

nre.description: NRE measures what fraction of the total connectivity deficit — weighted by magnitude — is addressed by the scaffold's channels.

conditions.name: EEG + DTI Guided
conditions.note: Channels routed by DTI trajectories, prioritized by EEG deficit magnitude.

conditions.name: Anatomy-Only
conditions.note: Channels follow straight lines through cavity, no functional weighting.

conditions.name: Random-Pore
conditions.note: Baseline: isotropic scaffold with no directional preference.

nre.footnote: All three conditions use identical total channel volume. Differences in NRE are due to channel orientation and prioritization, not quantity.
